content: We can borrow the graded vocabulary reflection learning framework from high school English curricula to design a structured hierarchical self-reflection mechanism for large language models, where LLMs iteratively refine their understanding of reasoning tasks just as human students deepen their grasp of a concept through repeated, layered reflection practice. This framework would grade LLM reflection outputs by how well they correct prior errors, mirroring how language teachers assess student reflection writing to build conceptual mastery.
